intro.title: Hadrian's Inspection
intro.body:
Hadrian travelled from Rome to Britannia to inspect the northern limes, leaving Antinous in Antioch. The journey took him across Gaul and the Channel; he walked the line where the empire would end.

At the frontier he ordered the great wall built — eighty Roman miles of stone across the neck of the island, marking not conquest but limit. A boundary in masonry. A frontier as policy.
